title: Faster is Opendoor's Moral Imperative as Revenue Falls
text: Opendoor CEO Emphasizes Speed as Company Reports Declining Revenue. Opendoor's newly appointed CEO Kaz Nejatian is prioritizing velocity in the company's operations, even as the iBuyer faces revenue headwinds. During the company's May 7 earnings call, Nejatian characterized speed as both a competitive advantage and an ethical obligation. Key Developments: The company reported Q1 revenue of $720 million, down from $736 million in the previous quarter and significantly lower than the $1.2 billion reported a year earlier. However, Opendoor narrowed its net loss to $173 million from $1.1 billion in Q4. Nejatian indicated the company is on track to reach breakeven by year-end. Operational Improvements: The company has made measurable progress in inventory management. Over 80% of homes acquired in October have been sold, and the percentage of inventory remaining on the market for 120-plus days dropped from 51% to 10% over two quarters. Opendoor entered into contracts on more than 5,000 homes during Q1—double Q4 levels and triple Q3 volumes.
